channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: EU Alleges Meta's Paid Ad-Free Option Violates Digital Competition Rules
item.description: European Union regulators claim that Meta Platforms is breaching the bloc's digital competition rules by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between viewing ads or paying to avoid them, sparking concerns over unfair market practices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla deliveries are expected to fall for the second straight quarter
item.description: Tesla‘s June-quarter deliveries likely fell 3.7%, the first time the top EV maker is set to post two straight quarters of decline, as it deals with stiff competition in China and slow demand due to a lack of affordable new models.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Mary Meeker says AI and higher education need to team up
item.description: Influential tech investor Mary Meeker published a new report Monday calling for the tech industry, government, and higher education to all work together to advance artificial intelligence during what she sees as an inflection point “of epic proportions.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: 10 voice scams to watch out for – and your quick action plan
item.description: Any potential scammer can copy a voice with cheap, accurate AI software in their bid to swindle you out of big money. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The year so far in AI: the biggest stories from ChatGPT to Gemini and what’s coming next
item.description: It's been a huge year for AI –so at the halfway point of the year, we've rounded up the major AI products and features released in 2024 so far.
item.dc:creator: erichs211@gmail.com (Eric Hal Schwartz) , TechRadar

item.title: EU finds Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model violates tech rules
item.description: The European Union said Meta’s “pay or consent” advertising model for European users violates the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), according to preliminary findings released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: D-Link routers are being hacked to steal customer passwords — but it says there is no patch
item.description: The still-popular D-Link device in question reached end-of-life long ago, and as such will not be getting a patch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nutanix hunts disgruntled VMware customers
item.description: The new Nutanix is virtually unrecognizable from the company that created the hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) market in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Supreme Court Halts Social Media Regulation Efforts in Texas and Florida Amid Political Tensions
item.description: A Supreme Court injunction has paused the implementation of social media regulations in Texas and Florida, following the suspension of then-President Donald Trump's accounts on Facebook and Twitter over controversial posts referencing the 2021 US Capitol attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday wiped lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at barring social media companies from banning users based on their political views, kicking the major First Amendment challenge down the road.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: US Supreme Court Halts Texas and Florida Efforts to Regulate Social Media Platforms
item.description: The US Supreme Court returns cases to lower courts, blocking challenges from trade associations seeking to regulate social media companies
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DoD Joint Staff GenAI Use Cases Revealed: 8 Internal Applications
item.description: The DoD's Joint Staff has outlined 8 internal use cases for GenAI, following a sprint led by Lt. Gen. Todd Isaacson, the CIO for the Joint Chiefs of Staff/J-6.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How to trace and block anonymous calls
item.description: Do you want to prevent scam calls? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how to block hidden IDs on your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba to cease data center operations in India and Australia
item.description: Alibaba’s cloud computing unit, otherwise known as Alibaba Cloud or Aliyun, has decided to cease data center operations in India and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How smaller businesses can reimagine brand building with generative AI
item.description: Generating and maintaining awareness is notoriously challenging for small businesses, often working with limited in-house resources and without the budget for external marketing support.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why smaller businesses need AI PCs to make hybrid work a success
item.description: As hybrid working increasingly becomes the norm, businesses must ensure their digital strategy is enabling high quality collaboration in order to succeed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What key features underpin best-in-class AI PCs?
item.description: As businesses seek to leverage AI in all aspects of their operations, it’s vital that IT leaders understand how this transformative moment will impact hardware. CIOs who aren’t actively investigating the possibilities risk being left behind.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Robot Boat Tackles Waterway Waste with Autonomous Trash Removal
item.description: Kurt Knutsson, tech expert, discusses Clearbot, an autonomous vessel for efficient marine waste removal, designed to tackle waterway pollution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 AI-powered apps that’ll actually save you time
item.description: You can’t open your eyes these days without seeing something about generative AI and all the reasons it’s, like, totally gonna revolutionize the way you work. And yet, call me surly, but most of the AI tools out there at this point seem far less impressive in practice than they do on paper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why (and perhaps how) Apple Intelligence will make money
item.description: We’ll leave it to future retrospectives to show us if it turns out be a good or a bad thing, but artificial intelligence will change everything, so of course Apple is building a business around it. That shouldn’t surprise anyone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 steps to improve analytics for data-driven organizations
item.description: When leaders say they want to be a data-driven organization, a key objective is empowering business people to use data, predictive models, generative AI capabilities, and data visualizations to improve decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: FCC Proposes Mandatory Cell Phone Unlocking Policy for Carriers
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) proposes a new rule requiring US carriers to unlock cellphones within 60 days of purchase, allowing consumers to switch networks without carrier restrictions
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: Mastering Password Management: Tips for Securing Your Digital Life
item.description: A staggering number of individuals struggle with managing their numerous passwords, highlighting the need for effective password management strategies to maintain digital security.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court Nears Conclusion of Pivotal Term with Decision on Trump's Immunity Imminent
item.description: As the Supreme Court approaches the end of its term, a critical decision on Donald Trump's immunity is looming, following a flurry of rulings that touched on various aspects of American society
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google opens access to 2 million context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro
item.description: Google has opened access to the 2-million-token context window of the Gemini 1.5 Pro AI model. The company also is giving developers access to code execution capabilities in the Gemini API and making the Gemma 2 model available in Google AI Studio. These announcements were made on June 27.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI success: Real or hallucination?
item.description: Ever consider how much of AI is a hallucination? Not how much AI output is hallucination, but rather how much of what we hear or read about it is genuine? Could it be that AI success is mostly hype, and that all the hype may be hurting the technology rather than helping? Enterprises need to get to t
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis Orders Conversion of Vatican Radio Territory into Solar Farm in North Rome
item.description: Rome, Italy - Pope Francis has decreed that a territory in northern Rome previously blamed for electro-smog due to Vatican Radio towers will now be converted into a solar farm to power Vatican City. This move follows the Vatican's commitment to the United Nations' renewable energy goals.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia Resists Paying $8M Ransom After Cyberattack Compromises National Data Center
item.description: The Indonesian government refuses to pay an $8 million ransom demanded by hackers after a cyberattack compromised the country's national data center, highlighting its commitment to cybersecurity and public safety.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
